title: Canonical research metadata and evidence trace repair

preflight.note: Stamped by `repopact new`; the work item was recorded before implementation began.

acceptance_criteria.text: Finding F-014 and capture 013 record the proposed-state adoption episode and trace the downstream pressure through decision 0023, work item 025, schema and CLI behavior, conformance, release, and adopter rollout evidence.

Canonical research metadata defines lifecycle states, benchmark task counts, hypothesis-to-study mappings, and threat identifiers so repeated facts in the paper, figures, protocols, and threat register are generated or machine-cross-checked rather than independently maintained.

The duplicate T7, missing T9 and T10, four-state lifecycle figure, future-provenance wording, 21-versus-24 task count, and H8-H10 versus H8-H13 protocol drift are repaired without changing the underlying claims.

Regression checks fail on a repeated threat identifier or a research document that contradicts canonical lifecycle, task-count, or hypothesis metadata; the paper, gap audit, findings register, and run log accurately distinguish closed from open gaps.
